cards.back: An array in which each element is accessed by a single index.

cards.front: What is the purpose of the 'new' keyword in C++ for arrays?
cards.back: To dynamically allocate memory for an array.

cards.front: What is the difference between a one-dimensional array and a pointer?
cards.back: A one-dimensional array stores multiple elements, while a pointer stores a single memory address.

cards.front: What is the syntax for initializing a one-dimensional array in C++?
What is the purpose of the 'sizeof' operator in C++ for arrays?
cards.back: To get the total size of an array in bytes.
